title: 7-day transition from kibble to fresh food
summary: Gradual transition prevents osmotic diarrhea and gastric upset. Use a single highly digestible protein (chicken or turkey) for the entire transition.

days.note: Watch stool — looser stool is normal, but firm should be the baseline by day 2.
If digestive issues appear, hold this ratio an extra day before advancing.
Begin tapering off any commercial food.
Full fresh diet. Introduce protein rotation only after 14–21 days of stable digestion.

tips: Add a teaspoon of plain canned pumpkin for the first 3 days to support digestion.
Hold off on supplements (calcium, fish oil) for the first 48 hours, then introduce one at a time.
If diarrhea persists more than 48 hours, return to the previous day's ratio and consult a vet.
Senior dogs and dogs with sensitive GI tracts may need a 10–14 day transition instead of 7.
